name: UDOO Vision
description: Pico-ITX x86 SBC built around an Intel Apollo Lake SoC with an onboard ATmega32U4 Arduino-Leonardo microcontroller, aimed at edge computer-vision projects.

manufacturer.comment: UDOO is SECO S.p.A.'s maker brand; the board is designed and assembled by SECO in Italy and sold under the UDOO label.

model.comment: Sold in two SKUs: VISION X5 (Atom x5-E3940) and VISION X7 (Atom x7-E3950).

release-date.comment: Announced and made available on the UDOO shop in May 2023.

status.comment: Both X5 and X7 SKUs still listed on the UDOO/SECO shop as of 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: UDOO publishes a schematics PDF but no PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license; per RESEARCH.md guidelines schematics-only does not qualify.

country-of-origin.comment: UDOO/SECO is headquartered in Arezzo, Italy; product page states 'Made with love in Italy'.

soc.comment: Two SoC SKUs are sold; the X7 ships the higher-binned x7-E3950 part.

cpu-cores.comment: Both x5-E3940 and x7-E3950 are quad-core.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Top SKU (X7) reaches 2.0 GHz turbo; X5 tops out at 1.8 GHz. Recorded the higher figure for the board family.

process-node.comment: Apollo Lake is fabricated on Intel's 14 nm process.

gpu.value: Intel HD Graphics 500 (Gen9, 12 EU on X5 / 18 EU on X7)

opengl-es-version.comment: Intel Gen9 graphics support OpenGL ES 3.2 via the Mesa i965/iris driver; not a UDOO-specific claim.

vulkan-support.comment: Intel HD Graphics 500 (Gen9) supports Vulkan 1.x via the mainline Mesa ANV driver.

npu.comment: Apollo Lake has no dedicated NPU; UDOO markets 'AI' through OpenVINO running on the iGPU/CPU.

hw-video-decode.comment: Apollo Lake's Gen9 media engine decodes H.264, H.265 (HEVC up to 4K) and VP9 in hardware. AV1 is not supported.

hw-video-encode.comment: Apollo Lake Gen9 hardware encoders cover H.264 and H.265; no AV1 / VP9 encode.

ram-min-gb.comment: Smallest config (X5) ships with 4 GB LPDDR4 soldered.

ram-max-gb.comment: Top config (X7) ships with 8 GB LPDDR4 soldered; not user-upgradeable.

emmc.comment: 32 GB eMMC on X5, 64 GB on X7.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 Key B 2242/3042/2280 slot is wired for SATA SSDs only, not NVMe (no PCIe lanes routed to that slot).

sata.comment: 1× SATA III 6 Gb/s connector plus the SATA-mode M.2 Key B slot.

dual-ethernet.comment: Two GbE ports on the rear I/O.

wifi.comment: No Wi-Fi soldered on board; an M.2 Key E slot accepts an optional Wi-Fi/BT card (sold separately), so the bare board ships without wireless.

bluetooth.comment: No Bluetooth soldered on board; comes only with the optional M.2 Key E Wi-Fi/BT card.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: 2× USB 3.0 Type-A on the rear I/O.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: There are 2× USB 2.0 lanes but only on an internal pin header, not USB-A sockets.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: No USB-C connectors on the board.

poe-support.comment: Powered via 12 V DC barrel jack; no PoE support documented.

hdmi-outputs.comment: No native HDMI port. Video outputs are eDP (internal) and miniDP++; HDMI is only reachable via a passive/active miniDP-to-HDMI adapter.

max-display-resolution.comment: eDP supports 3840×2160 @60 Hz; miniDP++ tops out at 2560×1600 @60 Hz natively, 4K via active DP-to-HDMI adapter.

displayport.comment: 1× miniDP++ multimode DisplayPort plus 1× eDP (internal panel) connector.

mipi-dsi.comment: No MIPI-DSI connector documented; embedded display uses eDP instead.

mipi-csi.comment: No MIPI-CSI camera connector documented; cameras attach via USB or M.2.

audio-jack.comment: TRRS combo connector (mic + headphone) driven by a Cirrus Logic CS4207 codec.

gpio-pins.comment: 26-pin GPIO header carrying the ATmega32U4 (Arduino Leonardo) pinout: up to 23 digital I/O (7 PWM) and 12 analog inputs. Not the 40-pin RPi pinout.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Header is Arduino-Leonardo compatible, not Raspberry Pi 40-pin GPIO compatible.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Pico-ITX form factor with Arduino-Leonardo header; no RPi HAT mechanical or electrical compatibility.

pcie-lanes-exposed.value: 1× M.2 Key E (Wi-Fi/BT) + 1× M.2 Key B (WWAN)
pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: PCIe is routed to the M.2 Key E (Wi-Fi/BT) and the M.2 Key B WWAN slot. The M.2 Key B SSD slot is SATA-only. UDOO does not publish PCIe generation; Apollo Lake exposes PCIe 2.0 lanes.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI and UART are all available via the ATmega32U4 GPIO header / front-panel connectors.

idle-power-w.comment: UDOO does not publish idle power; no credible measured value found in third-party reviews. Per RESEARCH.md guidance, prefer null over guessing from TDP.

load-power-w.comment: Vendor lists SoC TDP only (9.5 W X5 / 12 W X7); whole-board load power is not published and no measured number was found. Marked null.

cooling-required.comment: Board ships with a heatsink + fan assembly attached to the SoC underside.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard RTC battery connector with a coin-cell battery.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Pico-ITX standard mounting pattern, not RPi-compatible.

weight-g.comment: Bare-board weight not published by UDOO; no credible third-party measurement found.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Standard x86_64 board with UEFI; Apollo Lake platform support has been mainline in the Linux kernel since 4.x. Generic distros boot directly via UEFI without any UDOO-specific patches.

official-os-image.comment: UDOO does not publish a board-specific Linux image; the docs instead point users to install stock Ubuntu/other x86_64 distros via UEFI.

distros-with-images.comment: No board-specific images, but UDOO docs explicitly walk through installing generic Ubuntu (22.04 / 20.04 for OpenVINO) and the board boots any standard x86_64 distro (Debian, Fedora, etc.). Listed only the ones UDOO names directly.

bootloader.comment: AMI UEFI firmware; standard x86 boot path.

long-term-support.comment: UDOO does not publish a stated software-support window; prior UDOO X86 boards have received multi-year UEFI updates but Vision-specific commitment is undocumented.

android-support.comment: Marketing copy on udoo.org/udoo-vision/ mentions 'Linux, Android and Windows', but UDOO publishes no Vision-specific Android image and the Operating Systems documentation only covers Linux and Windows. Marked false to avoid overstating support.

msrp-base-usd.comment: VISION X5 launch price USD 349 ex VAT; checked 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: VISION X7 launch price USD 419 ex VAT; checked 2026-04-26.

available-regions.comment: Sold worldwide via the official UDOO/SECO shop, which ships from Italy. Mouser distribution (datasheet listing) provides North-America reach.

typical-lead-time.comment: UDOO shop quotes 2–5 working days dispatch from Italy; checked 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive online docs portal, datasheet on Mouser, and a public schematics PDF; minor gaps (some docs pages 404, no published power figures) keep it short of 5.

community-size.comment: Active UDOO forum but Vision-specific traffic is modest; smaller than Raspberry Pi / Orange Pi communities.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run UDOO forum has dedicated UDOO Vision threads with regular activity.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: UDOO offers a small set of branded add-ons (Wi-Fi/BT M.2, WWAN modules, cases) but the Arduino-Leonardo header limits HAT/shield reuse and there is no broad third-party accessory ecosystem.
